summary: Phase 2 BlackieFi 3.0 microservices testing complete. Verified existing monolith backend APIs work correctly (18/18 tests passed). Validated new microservices code syntax (Entity, Portfolio, Assets services all pass Python linting and import checks). Frontend login and dashboard work correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: New microservices (entity, portfolio, assets) have clean code structure with proper async/await patterns
All three services use consistent patterns: lifespan management, user context extraction from headers, entity access verification
Entity service (673 lines): Full CRUD for entities, default entity management, entity summary endpoints
Portfolio service (898 lines): Accounts, investment vehicles, holdings with computed stats
Assets service (1269 lines): General assets, real estate, tax liens, private equity, precious metals with depreciation calculations
All services properly exclude MongoDB _id by using string IDs in serialization
Services are designed to run via docker-compose.microservices.yml but are NOT yet deployed in preview environment
seed_data_creation: None - used existing demo user and data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 microservices code created but NOT deployed. Only monolith is running in preview. New services (entity, portfolio, assets) at /app/services/ are ready for docker-compose deployment. All existing monolith endpoints tested: /api/health, /api/auth/login, /api/entities, /api/accounts, /api/assets, /api/categories, /api/transactions, /api/budgets, /api/goals, /api/debts, /api/investments/vehicles, /api/investments/holdings, /api/bills, /api/notifications
